decisions.what: Two-tier architecture: rules first, AI fallback
decisions.why: Matches the design doc exactly and keeps costs down by only calling Claude for ambiguous events
Lets the caller (fleet-watch-github poller) branch on $? without parsing JSON
Keeps config external and editable without modifying the script

discoveries: jq substring slicing works as .[0:500] for truncating body text
summary: Built fleet-watch-triage, a 113-line bash script that classifies GitHub events using two tiers: rule-based pattern matching (P0-P2 keywords, CI failures, trusted authors) and AI fallback via claude --print. Outputs triage JSON and exit codes for the caller.
